channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Apr 6, 16:44 UTCMonitoring - We have applied a fix, and are seeing success rates recover across affected services. We are continuing to monitor to ensure there is no recurrence of issues.Apr 6, 16:17 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Apr 6, 15:54 UTCUpdate - This issue...

item.title: The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Within Silicon Valley’s orbit, an AI-fueled jobs apocalypse is spoken about as a given. The mood is so grim that a societal impacts researcher at Anthropic,...

item.title: Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents
item.description: A vulnerability named ‘AI Agent Traps’ allows attackers to manipulate, deceive, and exploit visiting agents via malicious web content. The post Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Digital experience Logpush delay
item.description: Apr 6, 15:18 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare One customers that use LogPush to ingest DEX Device State Events will see up to a 20 minute delay. DEX data in the dashboard and the API is not impacted

item.title: Delay in some zone configuration deployment
item.description: Apr 6, 15:00 UTCResolved - We have resolved an issue that caused a minor delay in configuration deployments across a small number of zones between 14:56 and 20:36 UTC on April 5, 2026.

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no longer lives on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux infrastructure, and mobile devices, taking advantage of the fact that many...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More
item.description: This week had real hits. The key software got tampered with. Active bugs showed up in the tools people use every day. Some attacks didn’t even need much effort because the path was already there. One weak spot now spreads wider than before. What starts small can reach...

item.title: How LiteLLM Turned Developer Machines Into Credential Vaults for Attackers
item.description: The most active piece of enterprise infrastructure in the company is the developer workstation. That laptop is where credentials are created, tested, cached, copied, and reused across services, bots, build tools, and now local AI agents. In March 2026, the TeamPCP...

item.title: Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages
item.description: Hackers published 36 NPM packages posing as Strapi plugins to execute shells, escape containers, and harvest credentials. The post Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers
item.description: The threat actor behind the Axios supply chain attack has been aiming at other maintainers in its social engineering campaign. The post North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
item.description: For years Mike McClary sold the Guardian LTE Flashlight, a heavy-duty black model, online through his small outdoor brand. The product, designed for brightness and durability, became one of his most popular items ever. Even after he stopped offering it around 2017, customers kept sending him emails...

item.title: GET /v1/responses endpoint is currently down and unable to serve requests
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Responses (Operational)

item.title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
item.description: Threat actors associated with Qilin and Warlock ransomware operations have been observed using the bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) technique to silence security tools running on compromised hosts, according to findings from Cisco Talos and Trend Micro. Qilin...

item.title: Fortinet Rushes Emergency Fixes for Exploited Zero-Day
item.description: The improper access control bug in FortiClient EMS allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely. The post Fortinet Rushes Emergency Fixes for Exploited Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Some users may experience empty response from ChatGPT in web
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: BKA Identifies REvil Leaders Behind 130 German Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identity of the main threat actors associated with the now-defunct REvil (aka Sodinokibi) ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation. The threat actor, who went by the alias UNKN, functioned as a...

item.title: Delayed Cloudflare Page Shield data updates
item.description: Apr 6, 04:52 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is currently investigating an issue where data updates of the Page Shield product are delayed. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Holos: A Web-Scale LLM-Based Multi-Agent System for the Agentic Web
item.description: arXiv:2604.02334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models (LLM)-driven agents transition from isolated task solvers to persistent digital entities, the emergence of the Agentic Web, an ecosystem where heterogeneous agents autonomously interact and co-evolve, marks a pivotal shift...

item.title: Xpertbench: Expert Level Tasks with Rubrics-Based Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.02368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit plateauing performance on conventional benchmarks, a pivotal challenge persists: evaluating their proficiency in complex, open-ended tasks characterizing genuine expert-level cognition. Existing frameworks...

item.title: Compositional Neuro-Symbolic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.02434v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study structured abstraction-based reasoning for the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) and compare its generalization to test-time approaches. Purely neural architectures lack reliable combinatorial generalization, while strictly symbolic...

item.title: Understanding the Nature of Generative AI as Threshold Logic in High-Dimensional Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.02476v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper examines the role of threshold logic in understanding generative artificial intelligence. Threshold functions, originally studied in the 1960s in digital circuit synthesis, provide a structurally transparent model of neural computation: a...

item.title: AIVV: Neuro-Symbolic LLM Agent-Integrated Verification and Validation for Trustworthy Autonomous Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.02478v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models excel at detecting anomaly patterns in normal data. However, they do not provide a direct solution for anomaly classification and scalability across diverse control systems, frequently failing to distinguish genuine faults from...

item.title: Convolutional Surrogate for 3D Discrete Fracture-Matrix Tensor Upscaling
item.description: arXiv:2604.02335v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modeling groundwater flow in three-dimensional fractured crystalline media requires accounting for strong spatial heterogeneity induced by fractures. Fine-scale discrete fracture-matrix (DFM) simulations can capture this complexity but are...

item.title: Generating Counterfactual Patient Timelines from Real-World Data
item.description: arXiv:2604.02337v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Counterfactual simulation - exploring hypothetical consequences under alternative clinical scenarios - holds promise for transformative applications such as personalized medicine and in silico trials. However, it remains challenging due to...

item.title: LiME: Lightweight Mixture of Experts for Efficient Multimodal Multi-task Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.02338v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: MoE-PEFT methods combine Mixture of Experts with parameter-efficient fine-tuning for multi-task adaptation, but require separate adapters per expert causing trainable parameters to scale linearly with expert count and limiting applicability to...

item.title: SIEVE: Sample-Efficient Parametric Learning from Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2604.02339v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language context-such as instructions, knowledge, or feedback-contains rich signal for adapting language models. While in-context learning provides adaptation via the prompt, parametric learning persists into model weights and can improve...

item.title: Not All Denoising Steps Are Equal: Model Scheduling for Faster Masked Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.02340v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in masked diffusion language models (MDLMs) narrow the quality gap to autoregressive LMs, but their sampling remains expensive because generation requires many full-sequence denoising passes with a large Transformer and, unlike...

item.title: Using LLM-as-a-Judge/Jury to Advance Scalable, Clinically-Validated Safety Evaluations of Model Responses to Users Demonstrating Psychosis
item.description: arXiv:2604.02359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: General-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming widely adopted by people for mental health support. Yet emerging evidence suggests there are significant risks associated with high-frequency use, particularly for individuals suffering from...

item.title: CIPHER: Conformer-based Inference of Phonemes from High-density EEG
item.description: arXiv:2604.02362v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decoding speech information from scalp EEG remains difficult due to low SNR and spatial blurring. We present CIPHER (Conformer-based Inference of Phonemes from High-density EEG Representations), a dual-pathway model using (i) ERP features and (ii)...

item.title: SWAY: A Counterfactual Computational Linguistic Approach to Measuring and Mitigating Sycophancy
item.description: arXiv:2604.02423v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models exhibit sycophancy: the tendency to shift outputs toward user-expressed stances, regardless of correctness or consistency. While prior work has studied this issue and its impacts, rigorous computational linguistic metrics are...

item.title: Skeleton-based Coherence Modeling in Narratives
item.description: arXiv:2604.02451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modeling coherence in text has been a task that has excited NLP researchers since a long time. It has applications in detecting incoherent structures and helping the author fix them. There has been recent work in using neural networks to extract a...

item.title: Single-Agent LLMs Outperform Multi-Agent Systems on Multi-Hop Reasoning Under Equal Thinking Token Budgets
item.description: arXiv:2604.02460v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work reports strong performance from multi-agent LLM systems (MAS), but these gains are often confounded by increased test-time computation. When computation is normalized, single-agent systems (SAS) can match or outperform MAS, yet the...

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer...

item.title: SOC alerting for Core applications was impacted
item.description: Apr 6, 01:59 UTCResolved - Between 16:11 UTC on April 5 and 00:14 UTC on April 6, an error impacted SOC alerting for Core applications. The issue has been resolved, and all systems are now fully operational. No ongoing impact is expected.

item.title: Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 elevated error rate
item.description: Apr 4, 17:30 UTCResolved - Between 10:09 and 10:31 PDT, Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 experienced elevated error rates across most product surfaces.

item.title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Hackers stole over 300GB of data from the Commission’s AWS environment, including personal information. The post European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Queue resource error
item.description: Apr 3, 23:57 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Apr 3, 23:53 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of errors for customers running updates to Queues via Terraform.

item.title: [AINews] Good Friday
item.description: a quiet day.

item.title: Elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Apr 3, 19:21 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 3, 18:12 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue resulting in elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. In January, Elon Musk’s SpaceX filed an application with the US Federal Communications Commission to launch up to one...

item.title: Marc Andreessen introspects on The Death of the Browser, Pi + OpenClaw, and Why "This Time Is Different"
item.description: The legend needs no intro... if you pardon our pun

item.title: Gemma 4 and what makes an open model succeed
item.description: Hint: it's not benchmark scores.

item.title: Genspark Raises $385 Million, Accelerates Commercialization of AI Agent Platform
item.description: Genspark has raised $385 million at a $1.6 billion valuation, as its AI Agent platform gains traction in Japan with strong user engagement. The company is scaling its “AI Workspace” to accelerate enterprise adoption.

item.title: Mizzen Insight Raises Nearly $10 Million as AI User Research Platform Scales Rapidly
item.description: Mizzen Insight raised nearly $10 million to scale its AI-powered user research platform, cutting research cycles from weeks to under a day while serving clients like AlibabaGroup and Xiaomi.

item.title: EVE Energy Reports 2025 Revenue Growth as Multi-Segment Strategy Supports Expansion
item.description: EVE Energy reported $8.5 billion in 2025 revenue, driven by strong growth in both power and energy storage batteries, supported by global expansion and diversified operations.

item.title: LONGi Launches Energy Storage Strategy, Targets Overseas Markets and System Integration
item.description: LONGiSolar launched its “LONGi ONE” strategy to expand into energy storage, focusing on system integration and overseas markets with expected returns by 2027–2028.

item.title: UBTECH Offers Up to $17 Million Salary to Hire Chief Scientist as Humanoid Robot Revenue Grows
item.description: UBTECHRobotics is offering up to $17 million annually to hire a chief scientist, as humanoid robots become its largest revenue driver with over 1,000 units sold in 2025.

item.title: [AINews] Gemma 4: The best small Multimodal Open Models, dramatically better than Gemma 3 in every way
item.description: A welcome update from Google!

item.title: Drift-Resilient Temporal Priors for Visual Tracking
item.description: Temporal information is crucial for visual tracking, but existing multi-frame trackers are vulnerable to model drift caused by naively aggregating noisy historical predictions. In this paper, we introduce DTPTrack, a lightweight and generalizable module designed to be seamlessly integrated into...

item.title: Communication-free Sampling and 4D Hybrid Parallelism for Scalable Mini-batch GNN Training
item.description: Graph neural networks (GNNs) are widely used for learning on graph datasets derived from various real-world scenarios. Learning from extremely large graphs requires distributed training, and mini-batching with sampling is a popular approach for parallelizing GNN training. Existing distributed...
